message:
fix(world-gfx): remove texture cloning from RenderFrame, simplify readback API

## Overview

This commit fixes compilation errors in the world-gfx renderer by removing the non-cloneable wgpu::Texture from RenderFrame and simplifying the readback copy API to take texture reference directly.

---

## Changes

**RenderFrame simplification** (crates/scriptbots-world-gfx/src/lib.rs:45-47):
- Removed `pub color: wgpu::Texture` field (not cloneable)
- RenderFrame now only carries `extent: (u32, u32)`
- Texture ownership remains in WorldRenderer

**Readback API update** (crates/scriptbots-world-gfx/src/lib.rs:118-119):
```rust
// copy_to_readback no longer needs RenderFrame parameter
pub fn copy_to_readback(&mut self, _frame: &RenderFrame) -> Result<(), String> {
    self.readback.copy(&self.device, &self.queue, &self.color)
}
```

**ReadbackRing.copy signature** (crates/scriptbots-world-gfx/src/lib.rs:182):
- Takes `color: &wgpu::Texture` reference directly
- No longer needs to extract texture from RenderFrame

---

## Rationale

wgpu textures cannot be cloned. The previous design tried to return owned texture in RenderFrame, causing compilation error. The simplified design keeps texture ownership in WorldRenderer and passes references where needed.

---

## Files Changed

- `crates/scriptbots-world-gfx/src/lib.rs`: +4 lines, -10 lines
